description: Unlock modern C++ features (C++11/14/17) like Smart Pointers, Lambdas, and Move Semantics for safer and faster code.

slides.title: Modern C++ (C++11/14/17)
slides.content:
# The New Era 🚀

C++ has evolved significantly. Modern C++ focuses on **Safety** and **Performance**.

## Key Features
- Smart Pointers (No more memory leaks!)
- Lambda Expressions
- Auto Type Inference
- Move Semantics

slides.title: Smart Pointers: Unique
slides.content:
# Owning It 🔒

**`std::unique_ptr`**
- Represents **exclusive ownership**.
- Cannot be copied, only moved.
- Automically deletes the object when it goes out of scope.

```cpp
#include <memory>

unique_ptr<int> p1(new int(5));
// unique_ptr<int> p2 = p1; // ERROR: Can't copy
unique_ptr<int> p2 = move(p1); // OK: Ownership transferred
```

slides.title: Smart Pointers: Shared
slides.content:
# Sharing is Caring 🤝

**`std::shared_ptr`**
- Allows multiple pointers to own the same resource.
- Uses a **Reference Counter**.
- Object is deleted only when the LAST shared_ptr is destroyed.

```cpp
shared_ptr<int> p1 = make_shared<int>(10);
shared_ptr<int> p2 = p1; // Counter = 2
p1.reset(); // Counter = 1, object still alive
```

slides.title: Memory Safety
slides.content: What happens if you try to copy a `unique_ptr`?

slides.title: Lambda Expressions
Move Semantics
slides.content:
# Moving vs Copying 📦

Copying large objects is slow. **Move Semantics** allow resources (like memory) to be *transferred* from a temporary object to a new one.

- Uses **R-value references** (`&&`).
- `std::move(obj)` casts `obj` to an r-value, enabling the move constructor.

Critically important for performance in vectors and containers.

slides.title: Auto Keyword
Full Example: Modern C++
Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Advanced C++ 🎓

Modern C++ is safer and more expressive.

## Key Takeaways
- **Smart Pointers:** `unique_ptr` and `shared_ptr` handle memory for you.
- **Lambdas:** Inline functions for cleaner code.
- **Auto:** Saves typing complex types.
- **Move Semantics:** Optimizes performance by avoiding deep copies.

You have now covered the spectrum of C++ from basics to advanced features! Keep coding! 🚀
